LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PLEAD THAT CLAIM AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY SUCH AS DEFENDANT AS A MATTER OF LAW. SEE MIKLOSY V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, 44 CAL.4TH 876; ANTHOINE V. N. CENT. COUNTIES CONSORTIUM (9TH CIR. 2010) 605 F.3D 740. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR PARTIAL JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. PLAINTIFF FILED A STATEMENT OF NON-OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077.
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Continued To Feb-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Set For Trial On Nov-12-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terence J. Howzell, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3960, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff filed a statement of non-opposition to this motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terence J. Howzell, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3960, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to the second cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot plead that claim against a public entity such as defendant as a matter of law. See Miklosy v. Regents of University of California, 44 Cal.4th 876; Anthoine v. N. Cent. Counties Consortium (9th Cir. 2010) 605 F.3d 740. One order signed for both motions. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terence J. Howzell, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3960, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE portions of the COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend per Government Code 818. One order signed for both motions. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
